# Avoid Cellphone Use on Stairs
[Image: Avoid Cellphone Use on Stairs]
**Friday, April 17, 2026**
**Safety Reminder Regarding Cellphone Use on Stairways**  
To ensure the safety of all students and faculty, we are reminding everyone of the following guideline regarding electronic device usage on stairs:
**Policy:**  
Cellphone use is strictly prohibited while ascending or descending staircases. This includes texting, browsing social media, making calls, or playing games.
**Rationale:**  
Stairways are high-traffic areas that require full attention. Using a cellphone while walking significantly reduces spatial awareness and reaction time, increasing the risk of:
* Slips, trips and falls
* Collisions with other students
* Blocking the flow of traffic during passing periods
**Procedure:**
* **Pause:** If you must send a message or answer a call, step to the side and stop walking before using your device.
* **Pocket:** Keep phones stored in pockets or bags while navigating steps.
* **Proceed:** Only resume walking once the device is put away and your full attention is on the stairs.
Thank you for helping us keep our campus safe.

# ONLINE APPLICATION for Gordon College Admission Test (GCAT)
[Image: ONLINE APPLICATION for Gordon College Admission Test (GCAT)]
**Thursday, November 27, 2025**
[Image: 📌]
**ONLINE APPLICATION for Gordon College Admission Test (GCAT)**
[Image: 📅]
First Semester A.Y. 2026–2027
[Image: 🗓️]
Application Period: November 27, 2025 – February 27, 2026
[Image: 📝]
GCAT Starts: January 26, 2026 – June 30, 2026
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
[Image: 📂]
**APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS**
• Senior High School ID or Any Valid ID Card
• PSA Authenticated Birth Certificate
• Recent Good Moral Character Certificate
• Academic Records
– Senior High School applicants: Grade 11 grades
– Transferees: Transcript of Records (TOR) or Certificate of Grades
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
[Image: 🖥️]
**APPLICATION PROCEDURE**
[Image: 🔺]
Basahin muna ang buong steps bago i-click ang portal link.
[Image: 🟢]
STEP 1. CREATE ONLINE PORTAL ACCOUNT
1. Go to the Gordon College Admission Portal
2. Click “Sign up”
3. Fill in: email, given name, last name, date of birth, contact number, desired password, and type of student (New, Transferee, Second Courser)
[Image: 📌]
Notes:
• Use a valid and existing email address.
• Make sure all details are correct.
• DO NOT create multiple accounts (ground for disapproval).
• Remember your email and password.
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
[Image: 🟢]
STEP 2. UPLOADING OF DOCUMENTS
1. Upload scanned copies or clear photos of the required documents (≤ 2MB each):
o Profile photo
– 2×2 colored picture, white background
– Formal pose, collared top
– No eyeglasses or accessories
– Taken within 6 months before filing
o Good Moral Character Certificate
o PSA Authenticated Birth Certificate
o Senior High School ID or Valid ID
o Academic Records (Grade 11 grades for SHS, TOR for transferees)
2. Click “Continue with Sign Up” to finalize.
Important: Always double-check to ensure that all documents have been successfully uploaded.
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
[Image: 🟢]
STEP 3. LOG IN TO YOUR ACCOUNT
1. Go to the portal login page.
2. Enter your registered email and password.
3. You will be directed to your Dashboard, which displays the status of your application.
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
[Image: 🟢]
STEP 4. PROFILING
1. On the Menu, click “Profile.”
2. Fill out all forms (Personal Info, Family Background, Desired Programs).
3. Read and check the Data Privacy Notice.
4. Review and click “Submit.”
5. Under Confirmation, click “Finalized Enlistment.”
o This step is required before you can proceed to Exam Scheduling.
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
[Image: 🟢]
STEP 5. GCAT SCHEDULE
• The schedule will be posted on your portal account.
• Once posted, you may download and print:
o GCAT Schedule Slip
o Student Information Sheet
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
[Image: 🟢]
STEP 6. APPLICATION INTERVIEW
• Within 3 days, your GCAT result will be posted in your portal.
• As soon as the results are posted, you will be scheduled for an interview by the Program Coordinator.
• Please come on the scheduled interview. The interview is part of the requirements in assessing if you will be accepted into the program.
• On the day of interview, bring the following:
o Copy of grades for Grade 12, 1st Semester or TOR for transferees.
o And other requirements as stated by the Program Coordinator.
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
[Image: 🟢]
STEP 7. CONFIRMATION OF ADMISSION
• After the interview, kindly check your portal to see if you are admitted to the program you have applied for.
• You have to click the “confirm” button in your portal. Clicking the “confirm” button will ensure that you will enroll at the college.
• After confirmation, stay tuned for announcements for Enlistment. prepare all requirements for enrolment.
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
[Image: 📌]
**WHAT TO BRING ON TEST DAY**
1. GCAT Schedule Slip
2. Student Information Sheet
3. 2×2 picture with white background
4. Senior High School or Valid ID Card
5. Pencil and Pen
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
[Image: ❗]
**Important Note:**
All applications shall be submitted online only. Please make sure to read and complete all steps before clicking the portal link.
[Image: 🔗]

# Ex-NSO prexy secures rank 9 spot; GC among top schools in Feb 2026 PNLE
[Image: Ex-NSO prexy secures rank 9 spot; GC among top schools in Feb 2026 PNLE]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, March 6, 2026**
Alumna Alexis Molon Penuliar of the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) ranked ninth in the February 2026 Philippine Nursing Licensure Examination (PNLE), as announced by the Professional Regulation Commission (PRC) today, March 6.
Graduated cum laude, the former Gordon College – Nursing Student Organization (GC-NSO) president earned an 89.80% rating in the recent board examination.
The institution recorded 55 passers out of 60 total takers, posting an overall passing rate of 91.67%. Comprising this are 55 out of 57 first-time passers while none of the three repeat examinees passed.
Moreover, GC was among the country’s top-performing schools at 4th rank in the recent licensure exam with 50 to 99 examinees and at least 85% passing percentage, far surpassing the national passing rate of 44.24%.
Meanwhile, Alyanna Marie Ramos Villegas of Saint Paul University – Tuguegarao (SPU-T) was the February 2026 PNLE’s Top 1 Passer, having 92.00% rating. SPU-T also emerged as the Top 1 Performing School with 100 or more examinees, having 99.10% passing rate with 220 successful examinees out of 222.
PRC administered the examination on February 26 and 27 in various testing centers, including Manila, Baguio, Cebu, Davao, and several key provinces such as Palawan, Laguna, and Batangas. | via Alyssa Marie Alcantara and Keane Rafael Abdon/The FOREFRONT

# TF marks history at 21st LHEPC; 3 scribes snatch indiv awards
[Image: TF marks history at 21st LHEPC; 3 scribes snatch indiv awards]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Sunday, March 22, 2026**
The Forefront (TF) delegates secured three individual awards, first time in the publication’s Luzon-wide stint, at the 21st Luzon-Wide Higher Education Press Conference (LHEPC) from March 17-19, at NGN Grand Hotel in Tuguegarao City.
Representing Central Luzon, the individual category participants bagged awards in: Traditional Literary Graphics Illustration (English), 3rd place, from Senior Layout Artist Angelique Jose; Head of Traditional Arts Mary Isobelle Arcilla secured 7th Place in Traditional Comic Strip Drawing (English); and John Christian Rocero, former social media manager, took home 9th place in Photojournalism (English).
At the same time, John Patrick Mateo, Editor-in-Chief, participated in Devcomm Writing (Filipino) while Lance Isaac Leon, Associate Editor, participated in Editorial Writing (English) and Column Writing (English).
Moreover, Region III was named the Best Performing Region for the Individual and Group categories.
Announced on the same day, LHEPC will be replaced next year by the 1st National Higher Education Press Conference (NHEPC), to be hosted by Region I at Vigan City.
Similar to LHEPC rules, the top 5 placers in each category from the respective regional presscons will advance to the NHEPC. | via Roi Chester B. Manio/ THE FOREFRONT

# Rights and Obligations of GCAAI Members
#### Rights of Members
All members, regular and honorary, who complied with all the requirements, guidelines, and policies set forth by the Association are members in good standing.
Every member in good standing shall have the following rights:
1. To attend the regular and special meetings of the general membership of the Association;
2. To vote and be elected into office;
3. To propose amendments and resolutions, and to vote on those presented at the meetings above mentioned;
4. To request access to official records, documents, and papers pertaining to the acts, transactions, or decisions of the Association unless otherwise specified;
5. To use the properties and facilities of the Association according to the rules and regulations agreed upon for this purpose;
6. Such other rights and privileges as may be granted by the Board of Trustees.
#### Article 4 Section 5 – The right to vote and be elected as Trustees
The right to vote and be elected as Trustees of this Association shall be exclusively granted to Active Members and members in good standing. Provided, they are listed in the SEC Membership Book/and other forms of registration conducted by the Association and shall be certified correct by the Corporate Secretary. Provided, further, that only active members shall be appointed to any position in the Association.
#### Article 4 Section 6
It is understood that those who have been accepted members of the Association prior to the effectivity of the present By-Laws shall continue to be active members. Provided, further, that after the first general assembly and election, they shall satisfy the requirements stipulated under Section 3.
#### Duties and Obligations of Members
1. Every member, whether regular or honorary, has the responsibility to abide in this Constitution and Bylaws, and rules and regulations set forth by the GC-AA.
2. Every member must pay membership fees and annual fees in an amount determined by the majority vote of the GC-AA Executive Committee and Board. All dues payments shall be made to the treasurer and/or duly representative of the Association.
3. Every member must support and promote the thrusts and objectives of the GC-AA and the College.
4. Every member must participate actively in all Gordon College activities and GC-AA programs and projects.
5. Every member must exercise his/her rights and do his/her responsibilities as a GC-AA member.
6. Every member shall be entitled to exercise full membership rights, including the right to vote and hold office within the Association in accordance with the By-laws.
#### Disciplinary Action Against Members
The GC-AA Board of Trustees shall review and act upon all disputes regarding infractions of Association rules and regulations. The Trustees can form an Ad Hoc Committee that can render decisions in terminating a member from the Association.
The final decision of the said committee shall become final upon the approval of the GC-AA Board of Trustees at its regular monthly or special meeting. The GC-AA Board may overrule the Committee by a two-thirds (2/3) vote.

# “2,000 change-makers” attend NSTP grad ‘25
[Image: “2,000 change-makers” attend NSTP grad ‘25]
[NSTP](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/nstp/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Sunday, June 8, 2025**
Two thousand Gordon College (GC) students enrolled in their National Service and Training Program (NSTP) courses filled the SMX Convention Center, Olongapo City, on Wednesday, for this year’s NSTP Graduation Ceremony.
With the theme, “Volunteerism in Action: Shaping Leaders, Strengthening Community”, this year’s batch of NSTP graduates was graced by Miss Manila 2014 Dr. Krischelle D. Halili.
Dr. Halili emphasized that leadership is an “act not for yourself, but for others” in her address. “Serve something greater than yourself and take your friends with you.”
Moreover, she called on students to love the country and God while also channeling ‘tech natives’ to participate in online community initiatives as well as community services and university council activities on-site.
Leading the initial confirmation of the graduates, Vice President for Academic Affairs Dr. Darwin P. Paguio also delivered his special message, focusing on the impact of genuine leadership.
“True leadership is not defined merely by title or accolades but by the impact you make in the lives of others,” he said as he congratulated the “two thousand change-makers” afterward.
This was followed by the awarding of certificates to Literary Training Service (LTS) and Civic Welfare Training Service (CWTS) completers from the different departments of the institution.
NSTP Instructor Mr. Hubert Lew Magrata delivered his closing remarks to officially close the program.

# Bagong Lakan at Lakambini ng Kasaysayan, pinasinayaan sa Gordon College
[Image: Bagong Lakan at Lakambini ng Kasaysayan, pinasinayaan sa Gordon College]
[CEAS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ceas/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Sunday, September 7, 2025**
GORDON COLLEGE—Inukit na ang mga bagong lakan at lakambini ng kasaysayan kasabay ng panapos na selebrasyon ng Buwan ng Kasaysayan 2025 na may temang “Diwa ng Kasaysayan, Kabilin sa Kabataan” na idinaos noong Agosto 30, sa Gordon College Physical Education (PE) Hall sa pangunguna ng Samahan ng mga Mag-aaral sa Araling Panlipunan (SMAP).
Nagwagi sina G. Lorenzen Rob Gonzales mula sa BSEd Soc 3-A at Bb. Angel Mae Deseo mula sa BSEd Soc 1-A para sa Lakan at Lakambini ng Kasaysayan 2025.
Nasungkit naman nina G. Jhan Girbaud Hipolito mula sa BSEd Soc 2-A at Bb. Jhade Aizelle Quiocho mula sa BSEd Soc 1-A ang korona bilang Ginoong Unang Gantimpala at Binibining Unang Gantimpala.
Namayagpag din bilang Ginoong Ikalawang Gantimpala at Binibining Ikalawang Gantimpala sina G. Rafael Ellado mula sa BSEd Soc 1-A at Bb. Gervy Sarmiento mula sa BSEd Soc 2-A.
Bukod sa Lakan at Lakambini 2025, iginawad din ang mga espesyal na parangal tulad ng Lunting Diwa at Best in Talent na parehong nakuha nina G. Jhan Girbaud Hipolito at Bb. Jhade Aizelle Quiocho.
Nakamit naman nina G. Lorenzen Rob Gonzales at Bb. Jhade Aizelle Quiocho ang mga titulong Best in Formal Attire at People’s Choice Award.
Samantala, inuwi naman nina Bb. Angel Mae Deseo at G. Lorenzen Rob Gonzales ang karangalang Binibini at Ginoo ng Nakaraan.
“Nung sinabi na ako yung panalo, naging proud ako kasi nagbunga yung suporta ng pamilya at mga kaibigan, higit sa lahat, nagbunga yung effort at tiwala ko sa sarili” saad ng Lakan 2025.
“In-expect ko talaga na may chance akong manalo.” Ibinahagi din ni Lorenzen na pinaghirapan niya ang kanyang preparasyon, mula ensayo hanggang pagpapakilala at pagsagot sa tanong ng mga hurado.
Samantala, labis naman ang pasasalamat ni Bb. Angel Mae Deseo, Lakambini 2025, sa lahat ng tumulong sa kanya lalo na at noong panahong iyon ay masama pa ang kanyang karamdaman.
“Tinrangkaso po ako hanggang sa mismong laban, sa kabila nun ay ipinagpatuloy ko ang laban sapagkat nakikita ko ang aking mga kamag-aral (SOC 1-A) na nagtutulong-tulong upang matapos ang lahat ng aming kakailanganin,” saad ni Angel Mae Deseo.
“Lahat ng pagod at puyat ay naging makabuluhan sapagkat nasungkit namin ang korona at karangalan,” aniya. | via Lovely Doroin/The FOREFRONT

# CEAS Rep crowned as the First-Ever Mr. Tourism Olongapo 2025
[Image: CEAS Rep crowned as the First-Ever Mr. Tourism Olongapo 2025]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, October 15, 2025**
“Lord, hindi ako magtatampo kung hindi mo ibibigay sa akin.”
This is the silent cry of Chester Pislaan, a 4th-year student from the College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) of Gordon College, before he was crowned as Mr. Tourism Olongapo 2025 during the grand coronation on October 10, 2025, held at the SMX Olongapo Convention Center, SM City Olongapo Central.
Pislaan expressed his plans to use his platform to promote sustainable fashion and empower student workers and breadwinners as the newly crowned Mr. Tourism Olongapo.
“I will focus on informing everyone to refuse fast fashion while also sharing my story, especially with student workers,” he highlighted his advocacy for both environmental and social awareness.
The CEAS Student has a strong will to win, but admitted that things turned upside down when he saw how well the other candidates performed, saying that he’s just like the other contestants— with a heart full of hope and dreams.
“He [Lord] gave me these titles not just for recognition but to expand my reach,” his sentiment after being announced as the winner. He described the moment as both surreal and purposeful, as he connected his earlier achievements as Ginoong Linggo ng Kabataan – Olongapo and Mr. Gordon College Siklab 2025 to a broader mission of service and inspiration.
In addition, Candidate #6 Marc Anthony Acula from the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA), earned the title of 2nd Runner-Up. Candidate #1 Jerichson Martorillas, a student from the College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS), brought pride to the competition by winning the Best in Talent Award.
Meanwhile, Candidate #7 Blair Ollague, also from CBA, and Candidate #3 Bobby Ken Alfonso Morata from College of Computer Studies (CCS), delivered an exceptional performance that highlighted their confidence, poise, and dedication, making both candidates standout figures of the night. | via Lovely Doroin/The FOREFRONT.

# From Debits to Dominance: Pink Team (cPINKa) Credits the Ultimate Gamble Victory
[Image: From Debits to Dominance: Pink Team (cPINKa) Credits the Ultimate Gamble Victory]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Sunday, March 1, 2026**
Accountancy students of Gordon College gathered at East Tapinac Covered Court on February 25 for Junior Philippine Institute of Accountants (JPIA)’s Ultimate Gamble Team Building.
The participants collaborated, competed, and conquered through strategy-driven games that tested teamwork and unity.
Demy Anne M. Roallos headed the Green Team (Berde Makausad), Triskelly Lianne Cyril Cabildo led the Red Team (Pulang Ina), Felicity Dyle Rivas guided the Yellow Team (Bibeni Luca), Aran Benz C. Sardañas captained the Orange Team (Ilocos Empanada), Shakira Nicole B. Guintu steered the Pink Team (cPINKa – Certified Pink Accountants), Jan Lester M. Bitangcol led the Purple Team (Ooh-Lila), and Hannah Jhem M. Malit directed the Blue Team (BIR – Bluereau of Impeccable Recorders).
As the event began, each team performed their energetic yells and presented their flags to start the event with a blast. In the first game entitled “Caterpillar Game,” Green Team (Berde Makausad) won first place, Orange Team (Ilocos Empanada) gained second place, and Yellow Team (Bibeni Luca) took third place.
For the second game of “Pass the Balloon,” Red Team (Pulang Ina) achieved the first place, followed by the Pink Team (cPINKa), and lastly, the Yellow Team (Bibeni Luca). As the third game commenced, entitled “The Floor is Lava,” Pink Team (cPINKa) was hailed as the champion, Green Team (Berde Makausad) as the first runner-up, and the Orange Team (Ilocos Empanada) as the second runner-up.
Moreover, in the station games, the teams played in seven stations, namely Pass the Ball, Cut it Up, Wrap-tastic, Balloon Cup Relay, Rollercoaster Ball, T-Shirt Relay, and Calcu Relay. Yellow Team (Bibeni Luca) triumphed in first place, Purple Team (Ooh-Lila) succeeded in second place, while Pink Team (cPINKa) acquired third place.
After consolidating all performance entries like preparing a final financial statement, Pink Team (cPINKa) emerged as the overall champion with a total audited score of 185 points, followed by Green Team (Berde Makausad) with 180 points, Yellow Team (Bibeni Luca) with 175 points, Blue Team (BIR) and Red Team (Pulang Ina) who tied with 170 points, and lastly, Orange Team (Ilocos Empanada) and Purple Team (Ooh-Lila) who both garnered 150 points. Furthermore, Blue Team (BIR) won for the best yell, while Red Team (Pulang Ina) claimed the best flag award.
According to Pink (cPINKa) team leader, Shakira Nicole B. Guintu, their victory came from proper planning before the activities and going all-in on collaboration. “No’ng nag-activity po kami, laging may nagsasalita and nakikinig. Pwedeng higher batch, pwedeng lower batch, basta lahat naman po kami nakikipag-cooperate,” said Guintu.
Guintu also added that the team building reflected real-world challenges in accountancy, helping them to be patient and to remain calm under pressure. “May activities na parang paulit-ulit na kami pero nire-remind namin na kalmahan lang, na i-go lang sa flow namin, sa pace namin,” the team leader stated.
The activity ultimately reinforced that future accountants must not only master numbers but also embody the 3 C’s—Collaborate, Compete, and Conquer—skills essential in navigating real-world professional engagements and organizational challenges. | via Justine Stephanie A. Canlas/The FOREFRONT

# OFFICIAL STATEMENT FROM GORDON COLLEGE
[Image: OFFICIAL STATEMENT FROM GORDON COLLEGE]
**Sunday, January 4, 2026**
Gordon College is aware of the report aired by Bilyonaryo News in January 2026 concerning an alleged ₱19 million overpayment related to the implementation of the Free Higher Education (FHE) program specifically the mismatched definition of laboratory rooms.
In connection with the Commission on Audit’s (COA) rejoinder dated March 14, 2025, regarding Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. CHED-CO-2025-003 concerning the Free Higher Education (FHE) reimbursements to Gordon College for Academic Years 2019-2020 to 2023-2024, the institution submitted its clarifications and justification in coordination with the UniFAST Secretariat and Commission on Audit last May 16, 2025.
The institution’s initial inclusion of the Audio-Visual Room (AVR) fees under laboratory fees was made with good intentions and full transparency. This long-standing policy was openly reflected in the institution’s submitted and approved Tuition and Other School Fees (TOSF), which listed the AVR for laboratory fees. However as early as last year, once informed that “laboratory fees” is strictly interpreted to relate to specific courses that use specialized equipment, which the previous classification may not have fully met, the College adjusted accordingly and removed the said fees from the list.
The College wishes to clarify that funds received under the Free Higher Education program are meticulously allocated and budgeted which undergoes strict audit by appropriate national government agencies, including the Commission on Higher Education (CHED) and UniFAST and the Commission on Audit. All collections approved by the Commission on Higher Education (CHED) – UniFAST were allocated transparently for the operations and development of the college.
Gordon College is fully cooperating with concerned government agencies and complying with the recommendations of the Commission on Audit. The institution has always upheld transparency, accountability, and the proper stewardship of public funds. We respectfully call on the public to allow due process to take its course and to rely on verified information from official sources. Gordon College remains committed to addressing this matter responsibly and will provide updates as soon as official findings are available.

# Implementation of the School Uniform Policy
[Image: Implementation of the School Uniform Policy]
**Wednesday, July 30, 2025**
𝐀𝐃𝐕𝐈𝐒𝐎𝐑𝐘
The implementation of the school uniform policy will begin on 𝐒𝐞𝐩𝐭𝐞𝐦𝐛𝐞𝐫 𝟖, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓, 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐅𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐡𝐦𝐞𝐧 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐟𝐞𝐫𝐞𝐞𝐬, while 𝐒𝐨𝐩𝐡𝐨𝐦𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐬 𝐭𝐨 𝐒𝐞𝐧𝐢𝐨𝐫𝐬 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐛𝐞 𝐫𝐞𝐪𝐮𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐝 𝐭𝐨 𝐰𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐢𝐫 𝐬𝐜𝐡𝐨𝐨𝐥 𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦𝐬 𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐀𝐮𝐠𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝟏𝟖, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓.
𝐖𝐨𝐫𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐒𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬 must accomplish their Uniform Exemption Pass from 𝐀𝐮𝐠𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝟏 𝐭𝐨 𝟏𝟓, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓 𝐚𝐭 𝐫𝐨𝐨𝐦 𝟏𝟐𝟐 at the office of our Student Discipline Officers.
The following shall 𝐧𝐨𝐭 𝐛𝐞 𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐝:
1. Sleeveless tops, spaghetti straps, razor backs, tubes, hanging or mid rib blouse
2. All types of shorts (walking shorts, cycling shorts, tokong) tight fitting (leggings) and torn or mutilated jeans
3. Clothes with frayed hems
4. Blouses and dresses with plunging necklines/backless
5. Garments that are backless and See-through attires
6. Miniskirts (more than 2 inches above the knee) except where curriculum requires.
7. Long Skirts with high slit
8. Jogging pants (allowed only for PE schedule)
9. Slippers (exemptions: medical reason, weather conditions)
10. T-shirts with vulgar, indecent, suggestive points, or anything which advertise or promote the use of drugs, alcohol, or tobacco.
11. Clogs (e.g., Crocs) may be worn if for medical reasons and during inclement weather (heavy rains and excessive heat).

# Gordon College Celebrates 26th Founding Anniversary with Successful FUNdation Run 2025
[Image: Gordon College Celebrates 26th Founding Anniversary with Successful FUNdation Run 2025]
[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [GCFAS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/gcfas/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, February 28, 2025**
Gordon College launched its 26th Founding Anniversary festivities through FUNdation Run 2025, which took place on February 23rd.
Gordon College Faculty Association (GCFAS), in partnership with Gordon College Alumni Association Inc. (GCAAI), successfully organized the event as many students, faculty members, alumni, and community members attended the celebration.
Among the distinguished guests present were Hon. Sarah Lugerna Lipumano-Garcia, Olongapo City Councilor; Dr. Imelda D.P. Soriano, College President; Ms. Agnes L. Melo, GCFAS President; and Mr. Joseph Angelo B. Pusing, GCAAI President.
They joined GCFAS and GCAAI officers, college officials, administrative staff, faculty, students, alumni, and numerous college supporters in making the event a resounding success.
Jeian Espiritu emerged as the winner in the Boys Student Category, completing the race in an impressive 10 minutes and 44 seconds.
“I was motivated to run by my teammates in the Gordon College athletics program and my girlfriend,” Espiritu shared. “Winning feels great but also adds pressure for future years.”
In the women’s Student Category, Susie May Mistica Ariño took first place with a time of 14 minutes and 43 seconds.
Ariño credited her partner’s encouragement and her focus on mental health for her success. “It started when my partner encouraged me to run,” Ariño explained.
“While running, I realized I wanted more than just a casual run for the sake of my mental health. I started joining his training plans until he gifted me a running watch.”
Furthermore, Nicole Bernal Marcial, an alumna of Gordon College, won the Alumni Category with a time of 14 minutes and 32 seconds.
“The FUNdation Run isn’t just about promoting fitness and health—it’s a way to celebrate and commemorate Gordon College’s 26th founding anniversary,” Marcial said.
“As an alumnus, I wanted to be part of this special occasion.”
He also emphasized the importance of community and the joy of running for his participation.
The winners also offered advice to aspiring runners.
Espiritu encouraged them to persevere even when facing challenges.
Meanwhile, Ariño stressed the importance of preparation and a winner’swinner’s mindset.
Lastly, Marcial advised runners to enjoy the process and find their motivation. “The hardest part of joining fun runs or any running event is finding the motivation to start. But once you understand why you’re doing it and learn to love the experience, everything else follows, ” he said.
He added, “One foot in front of the other, and always be proud of yourself for taking that step.”
The FUNdation Run 2025 served as a fitting start to Gordon College’s 26th Founding Anniversary, as it brought together the college community and promoted health and wellness.

# Hindi pula’t dilaw ang tunay na magkalaban
[Image: Hindi pula’t dilaw ang tunay na magkalaban]
[CAHS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/cahs/) [CBA](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/cba/) [CCS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ccs/) [CEAS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ceas/) [CHTM](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/chtm/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Sunday, September 7, 2025**
Gordon College, OLONGAPO CITY—Ibinandera nina Lourd Cedric Alejo ng College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) at Pauline Batinga ng College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) ang kani-kanilang kolehiyo matapos koronahan bilang bagong Lakan at Lakambini ng Buwan ng Wika 2025 noong ika-4 ng Sityembre.
Bukod sa prestihiyosong korona, itinanghal ding Lakan ng Larawan 2025 si Alejo habang nasungkit naman ni Rowena Ablian mula sa College of Allied Health Studies ang titulo ng Lakambini ng Larawan 2025.
Kinilala namang Lakan at Lakambini ng Pormal na Kasuotan sina Matthew Navarro (CHTM) at Shanelle Ferrer ng College of Computer Studies (CCS).
Batay sa kanilang ranggo, hinirang ang mga sumusunod:
Lakan at Lakambini ng Wika (1st Runner-up)
Matthew Navarro (CHTM) at Rowena Ablian (CAHS)
Lakan at Lakambini ng Panitikan (2nd Runner-up)
Henry Agustin Baja Jr. (CAHS) at Shanelle Ferrer (CCS)
Lakan at Lakambini ng Sining (3rd Runner-up)
Reyelle Ariza (CEAS) at Angel Mae Deseo (CEAS)
Lakan at Lakambini ng SamFil (4th Runner-up)
Clark Justin Pambid (CCS) at Luisa May Manlapat (CBA)
Bilang bahagi ng taunang pagdiriwang ng Buwan ng Wika, ang naturang patimpalak ay isinagawa sa pangunguna ng Samahang Filipino (SamFil). | via Keane Rafael Abdon/The FOREFRONT

# GC Welcomes 5 new Registered Midwifes, Perfect passing in November MLE
[Image: GC Welcomes 5 new Registered Midwifes, Perfect passing in November MLE]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, November 19, 2025**
Five alumni from the College of Allied Health and Services (CAHS) of Gordon College (GC) passed the Midwives Licensure Exam (MLE) according to the Professional Regulation Commission Board.
GC achieved a perfect passing rate of 100% with 5 passers out of 5 examinees, once again surpassing the rate of last year’s results of 93.48% and the national rate of 57.60%. They constitute the 2,152 newly registered midwives.
All are first-time takers of this year’s exam.
Additionally, Tagaloan Community College is the top-performing school in the licensure exam with a perfect passing rate from 85 examinees of the school.
Meanwhile, Princesa Dumale Talamayan, this year’s board topnotcher, from Palawan State University achieved a 94.00%.
The licensure exam was held on November 8-9 and 16. | via Roi Chester B. Manio/The FOREFRONT

# Paulino welcomes 9k studes with new building; rates GC as highly university-ready
[Image: Paulino welcomes 9k studes with new building; rates GC as highly university-ready]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Saturday, October 18, 2025**
GORDON COLLEGE MAIN CAMPUS — Coinciding with PAGLALAYAG 2025 (more commonly known as Apertura 2025), the newly acquired Gordon College Annex Building, located at Lower Kalaklan, Olongapo, was formally inaugurated on August 11.
In an interview with The Forefront, Olongapo City Mayor Atty. Rolen C. Paulino Jr. “wanted to be practical” in expanding Gordon College (GC), citing several important considerations.
Paulino believes that students applied to Gordon College for quality education, a main reason to attract 9,500 students this Academic Year (A.Y).
As the A.Y. 2025-2026 begins, the local chief executive highlighted their desire to make ongoing improvements to the current building and structure, ensuring new equipment is added as needed.
“We want to make sure that everything goes accordingly. Gusto talaga naming mag-expand,” the mayor said.
The back-to-back mayor of Olongapo City also considered improving and adding programs that would need more equipment and facilities.
“Tinitingnan din natin ‘yong possibility na mag-expand pa tayo kasi may clamor din to add additional [programs], whether more medical courses or engineering, lahat ‘yan pinag-iisipan namin,” the mayor added.
Now, the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) has officially moved to the newly inaugurated annex building of the institution.
When asked about the institution’s readiness for university status, the Board of Trustees Chairperson gave the institution a rating of 8 to 9 out of 10, stressing that while “the qualifications [are] there and personnel [are] there”, there remains the challenge of “accumulating enough resources to qualify.”
Moreover, the chairperson spoke of the institution being already at that point as “the quality of education [that Gordon College] provides is at par with some of the universities.”
“Sa results ng [board] exams natin, we’re always competing with the best universities,” he added.
Furthermore, the mayor underscored the need to instill professionalism and discipline among students through the institution, aiming to make them employable and ready to become future managers. He also nurtured and wished for the students to apply values of professionalism and intelligence to work hand-in-hand when enrolled within the institution.
“[Please] know that the city government is here to support the institution and the students. The city is always proud of Gordon College. It’s the home of many top-notchers, many successful community leaders, both in business and society. Mataas ang accreditation natin, we are internationally ranked, [and] we are very proud of the school and very proud of its students,” he concluded. | via Keane Rafael Abdon/The FOREFRONT
